title: Ornithidium patulum
intro: Ornithidium patulum, also known as the 'Tiger Orchid', is a species of epiphytic orchid native to Central and South America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its purported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Ornithidium patulum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other glycosides, which are thought to contribute to its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, Ornithidium patulum is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies and folk medicines in its native regions.
taxonomy: Ornithidium patulum is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It is native to Central America. The classification of Ornithidium patulum is further divided into the genus Ornithidium, and the species patulum. The genus Ornithidium is itself classified within the subtribe Maxillariinae. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its flowers and leaf structure.
